assertions.evidence: Two @TableTest annotations are present: testParseValidInputs and testParseInvalidInputs

assertions.text: Null input is represented as a blank cell (not the string 'null') in the table
assertions.evidence: Row 'Null input' has a blank Input cell: '| Null input            |        | null |'

assertions.text: The exception cases (empty string, letters-only, negative) are handled — via a Throws? column, assertThrows in the method body, or a separate @TableTest — not silently omitted
assertions.evidence: A separate @TableTest with a 'Throws?' column covers empty string, 'abc', and '-5.00' rows, each mapped to IllegalArgumentException

assertions.text: Valid inputs have a result column name ending with '?' (e.g. 'Money?' or 'Result?')
assertions.evidence: Valid inputs table has column 'Money?' as the output column

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: testParseValidInputs body: assertThat(Money.parse(input)).isEqualTo(expected); — testParseInvalidInputs body: assertThrows(expectedEx, () -> Money.parse(input)); — neither contains if or switch
